info: This sheet controls the entire website. You can change any text, photo, or number — and the website will update when you publish.
Change whatever you need in this spreadsheet (fix a typo, update a number, add a team member, etc.)
When you're happy with your changes, go to the menu bar at the top and click: LBR Website > Publish Changes
Wait about 30 seconds, then refresh the website. Your changes are live!
Nothing happens until you click Publish. You can make as many edits as you want first.
All the words on the website. Change the text in the "value" column. Leave the "key" column alone.
The people shown on the website. You can change names, titles, bios, and photos. Uncheck "active" to hide someone.
Each row is a classroom on the map. You can update teacher names, student counts, etc.
The dollar amount buttons on the donation page. Check "featured" on the one you want highlighted.
Organizations on the Partners page. Add a new row to add a partner, delete a row to remove one.
The pie chart on the homepage. Update the percentages when you have new financial data.
In the Site Content tab, the first column has things like "hero_headline" — don't change these. They're how the website knows where to put the text.
The very first row of each tab (key, value, name, etc.) — leave those as they are.
Don't rename the tabs at the bottom of the sheet.
Using the bold button (B) in Google Sheets does NOT make it bold on the website. You have to type special codes around the words instead.
Type before the word and after it. Example: 95% shows as bold 95% on the site.
Type before the word and after it. Example: all shows as italic all on the site.

Type
where you want the line to break. Example: "You can't learn
when you're hungry" puts each part on its own line.
Photos are stored as web links (URLs). To change a photo, paste a new link into the cell.
In Site Content, look for keys that end in _url or _image — those are photos.
Upload to Google Drive, set sharing to "Anyone with the link", then use the link. Ask Sibtain if you need help with this.
You may have accidentally changed something in the "key" column. Check that column — undo if needed (Ctrl+Z or Cmd+Z).
The link to the photo may be broken. Make sure the image URL still works by pasting it in your browser.
Did you click Publish? If yes, try waiting a minute and refreshing the page. Still nothing? Ask Sibtain.
Don't panic! You can always undo with Ctrl+Z (or Cmd+Z on Mac). Google Sheets also saves every version — go to File > Version history to go back in time.
Reach out to Sibtain for any website questions. For content questions, coordinate with John.
